commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f4e47f9ff75cc567dbb0441e6e7bd5e49dc023a6)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `openclaw.compat.pluginApi` compatibility checks for package installs, discovery, manifest-registry loading, docs, and regression tests.

PR surface: Source +195, Tests +444, Docs +18. Total +657 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of PR head shows `Object.assign` can retain `packageOpenClaw` across trusted fallback, and the linked user issue has live proof of the older-host plugin install failure this PR targets.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A focused automated repair can clear stale fallback metadata and add the missing regression; the broader fail-closed rollout remains a maintainer merge decision.

target.title: fix(plugins): reject incompatible package plugin API installs
target.body:
## Summary

- reject package installs when `package.json#openclaw.compat.pluginApi` requires a newer OpenClaw runtime than the current host
- preflight npm package metadata before mutating the managed npm root, so incompatible updates do not remove an existing installed plugin
- skip already-persisted non-bundled plugins at discovery/manifest-registry load time when their package `openclaw.compat.pluginApi` no longer matches the current host
- keep the direct package install path aligned with ClawHub's plugin API compatibility check, including OpenClaw alpha/beta/rc CalVer host labels on the same stable API floor
- preserve explicit prerelease API floor ordering, so `beta.1` does not satisfy a `beta.2` plugin API requirement
- check the package plugin API before validating current-host package extension shape, so a plugin built for a newer package shape reports the actionable compatibility error first
- document `openclaw.compat.pluginApi` as the OpenClaw-owned install compatibility contract

Fixes #85869

## Compatibility model

This PR uses `openclaw.compat.pluginApi` as the contract that answers: "can this OpenClaw runtime safely load this plugin package?" The host exposes a compatibility API version, and the plugin package declares the supported range. If the range does not include the host version, OpenClaw now fails before install mutation, dependency linking, or later runtime load.

This is intentionally separate from package version equality. OpenClaw and official plugins usually share CalVer release numbers, but version numbers alone are not a sufficient contract: a plugin can be newer than the installed host, prerelease floors need exact SemVer-style ordering, and package metadata is the place where the plugin can say which OpenClaw plugin API it needs. `openclaw.install.minHostVersion` remains a separate install UX floor, while `peerDependencies.openclaw` remains npm metadata and is not the OpenClaw install blocker.

## Selection behavior

This PR does not yet implement "pick the best compatible plugin version." For an npm spec such as `npm:@openclaw/whatsapp`, the resolver can still pick the registry's latest package; this patch makes that resolved package fail closed if its `openclaw.compat.pluginApi` is too new. That is the safe first step because it prevents older hosts from persisting a package that will crash later during plugin load.

The follow-up that makes most sense is a compatibility-aware resolver:

- ClawHub should answer "latest compatible for this host plugin API" server-side, using the same `openclaw.compat.pluginApi` metadata.
- npm fallback can walk package versions and read package metadata when ClawHub cannot provide a catalog answer.
- The selector should prefer the newest package whose plugin API range includes the host version, rather than assuming matching package/OpenClaw version numbers.
- `openclaw.build.openclawVersion` can remain provenance/debug metadata, not the primary compatibility contract.

## Motivation / root cause

`openclaw plugins install @openclaw/whatsapp` can resolve the latest npm package even on an older OpenClaw checkout. The install validator only enforced `openclaw.install.minHostVersion`, so a package whose `minHostVersion` floor still passed could be persisted even when its `openclaw.compat.pluginApi` required a newer runtime. The later plugin load then failed with an API mismatch such as `createSetupTranslator is not a function`.

The same compatibility field also needs to protect update and recovery paths. Npm installs can otherwise discover the package metadata only after the managed root has changed, and already-persisted external plugins can be loaded after a downgrade or state restore unless discovery applies the same package API gate.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Direct package installs now fail before extension validation, dependency linking, or persistence when `openclaw.compat.pluginApi` does not satisfy the current OpenClaw compatibility host version. Npm package installs now reject from registry metadata before managed-root mutation, and persisted non-bundled plugins with incompatible package API metadata are skipped during discovery and manifest-registry load.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout on macOS with Node v24.10.0, plus AWS Crabbox Linux live proof with Node v24.16.0 against the real npm registry. The live proof used an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME` and host compatibility version `2026.5.10-beta.1`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
CI=true OPENCLAW_HOME=<temp-openclaw-home> OPENCLAW_COMPATIBILITY_HOST_VERSION=2026.5.10-beta.1 node --import tsx src/index.ts plugins install npm:@openclaw/whatsapp
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/install.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/plugins/discovery.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --threads=1 src/plugins/install.ts src/plugins/install.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/install.ts src/plugins/install.test.ts
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/reference/RELEASING.md docs/plugins/manifest.md
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/reference/RELEASING.md docs/plugins/manifest.md
git diff --check
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
```

Evidence after fix: AWS Crabbox run `run_e0c2d1ab3025` downloaded the current `@openclaw/whatsapp@2026.5.27` package from npm and rejected it on the older host before persistence:

```text
plugin "whatsapp" requires plugin API >=2026.5.27, but this OpenClaw runtime exposes 2026.5.10-beta.1. Upgrade OpenClaw or install a compatible plugin version and retry.
```

The same Crabbox run then passed the focused plugin regression suite:

```text
Test Files 4 passed (4)
Tests 294 passed (294)
```

Observed result after fix: Older hosts now get an actionable compatibility error instead of persisting a package that can fail later during plugin load. The published WhatsApp package was not persisted on an older isolated host, incompatible npm updates leave an existing package directory and managed-root dependency unchanged, and persisted non-bundled plugins with incompatible package API metadata are skipped before package entry validation, and bundle-style package installs with future `openclaw.compat.pluginApi` metadata fail before copying.

What was not tested: A clean same-floor live registry install was not claimed; same-floor acceptance is covered by fixture proof. The full test suite was not run. The PR docs now also clarify that release sync bumps official plugin API floors by default, while plugin-only releases can intentionally keep a lower API floor when they support older hosts.

## Regression tests

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/clawhub.test.ts src/plugins/install.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/plugins/clawhub.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/clawhub.test.ts src/plugins/install.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/plugins/clawhub.test.ts src/plugins/discovery.test.ts src/plugins/channel-catalog-registry.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/discovery.test.ts src/plugins/channel-catalog-registry.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/install-source-utils.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/install.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.install.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry.test.ts src/infra/clawhub.test.ts src/plugins/clawhub.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/install.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/plugins/discovery.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/contracts/channel-catalog.contract.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/install-source-utils.ts src/plugins/install.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/install.ts src/plugins/install.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/plugins/manifest.ts src/infra/clawhub.ts src/infra/clawhub.test.ts src/plugins/clawhub.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/install.ts src/plugins/install.test.ts
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/reference/RELEASING.md docs/plugins/manifest.md
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/reference/RELEASING.md docs/plugins/manifest.md
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/plugins/manifest.md
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/plugins/manifest.md
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
git diff --check
```

## User-visible behavior

Operators get a clear install-time compatibility error that suggests upgrading OpenClaw or installing a compatible plugin version. Existing persisted plugins with incompatible package API metadata are skipped at load instead of failing later inside runtime code.

## Compatibility and risks

This intentionally makes `openclaw.compat.pluginApi` the OpenClaw-owned package install and non-bundled package load compatibility gate. It does not add version selection, so older hosts may still need users to install a specific compatible plugin version until the resolver learns "latest compatible" selection.

```bash
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/install.ts src/plugins/install.test.ts
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/reference/RELEASING.md docs/plugins/manifest.md
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/reference/RELEASING.md docs/plugins/manifest.md
```
```bash
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/plugins/manifest.md
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/plugins/manifest.md
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
git diff --check
```

## User-visible behavior

Operators get a clear install-time compatibility error that suggests upgrading OpenClaw or installing a compatible plugin version. Existing persisted plugins with incompatible package API metadata are skipped at load instead of failing later inside runtime code.

## Compatibility and risks

This intentionally makes `openclaw.compat.pluginApi` the OpenClaw-owned package install and non-bundled package load compatibility gate. It does not add version selection, so older hosts may still need users to install a specific compatible plugin version until the resolver learns "latest compatible" selection.
